The Football Transfer Sources Reliability Guide

Football Transfer Source Reliability Tiers

Tier 1 – Almost undisputed when discussing a Transfer Rumour. They may not be the first to discuss a rumour, but if they are talking about it; then it is almost certainly true, and possibly close to complete.
Tier 2 – Known to have current sources, but are still fallible and just short of being considered an authority on rumour discussions. However, multiple individuals or sources from this tier reporting the same rumour may indicate a strong possibility of truth to the rumour.
Tier 3 – Unreliable, and not to be trusted solo. They fall into near click-bait practices and are often fighting for relevance in a sea of rumours.
Tier 4 – Not to be trusted at all. These companies make money out of ensuring that you read them and they will make up anything to get you to harness advertising revenue.
Tier 5 – Don’t waste your time reading them

Juventus: Genoa pursuing for Pellegrini. The full-backs situation

After Pjaca, Genoa are again knocking to Juventus door for the left full-back Pellegrini, after his loan to Cagliari in the past season.

Genoa's second option for that role is Reca from Atalanta, but the club is pursuing for the Juventus player. Pellegrini has also other requests from Atalanta and Parma, and Juventus is pondering the decision: the other options as fullbacks - besides Cuadrado and Alex Sandro - are Danilo and De Sciglio, and the former Milan player is the main candidate to start on the left against Sampdoria on Sunday, after Alex Sandro's injury.

In case Pellegrini leaves the club, De Sciglio is going to stay.

La Voix du Nord report that Ligue 1 side Lille are still anticipating that French youth international midfielder Boubakary Soumaré will depart the club before the transfer window closes on October 5th.

Contrary to reporting last week, the player has no desire to return to PSG, but the outlet claims that AC Milan, Inter Milan and Napoli have all positioned themselves to sign the 21-year-old.

The outlet also claims that there is German and English interest in the 21-year-old, but does not expand further.

Soumaré is looking for a destination that provides him with European football.

Barcelona & AS Roma want Antonio Rüdiger on loan

According to Kicker, Barcelona and former club AS Roma are interested in taking 27-year-old central-defender Antonio Rüdiger on loan from Chelsea.

As reported by The Telegraph earlier today, the Germany international has fallen down the pecking order at Premier League club Chelsea and is no longer willing to accept his role at the London club with the European Championship just one year away.

Andreas Christensen, Thiago Silva, Kurt Zouma and Fikayo Tomori are all ahead of Rüdiger at Chelsea and if Rüdiger wants to keep his place in the Germany national team, he has to be playing.

Bayer Leverkusen enquired about Julian Brandt (Borussia Dortmund)

According to Sport1, Bayer Leverkusen enquired about former player Julian Brandt this summer, which was swiftly rejected by Borussia Dortmund.

BVB head coach Lucien Favre has made it clear that they’re looking to strengthen their squad and therefore, the departure of Germany international Brandt would be extremely unlikely.

The 24-year-old lost his place to Giovanni Reyna and Jude Bellingham in their 3-0 win against Borussia Mönchengladbach but remains a key component in Favre’s squad.

Fulham make €18m bid for Jean-Clair Todibo

RMC Sport report that Premier League newboys Fulham have submitted an €18m offer to Barcelona today to sign French central defender Jean Clair Todibo.

Ligue 1 side Rennes have in recent hours activated their network to attempt to sign the 20-year-old, with Sporting Director Florian Maurice having given the ex-Toulouse man a call last week.

Téléfoot Chaine report that SRFC are trying accelerate talks to close a loan with option to buy deal before competitors for his signature get moving. One to watch in the final days of this window…
